primary_discipline.level1: 电子、通信与自动控制技术

evidence.original_text: UAVSig: UAV RF dataset with time-frequency labels. Wideband, multi-channel data. Multiple transmitters of the same models. Scenario = one combo of specific TX(s) and channel parameters. Two capture days (5/9/24 and 5/10/24). Full dataset (~222 GB) available at: https://cores.ee.ucla.edu/downloads/datasets/uavsig/

Receiver: USRP B205mini SDR. 20 dBi panel antenna with an 18° beamwidth. 6 ft coax cable between Rx and antenna with no additional LNA. Captures: 6, 1s captures per scenario. 50 MHz bandwidth. 2.4435 GHz center frequency – slightly offset from the perfect center of the four, 10 MHz drone channels. DJI controllers’ 1 MHz signals hop over 2.4-2.4835 GHz (ISM band).
4 DJI Matrice 100 drones and controllers. Campus building neighboring roofs. RX antenna angled upward on the upper roof to avoid interference.

Iterative signal detection algorithm. 1. Time-domain duration estimation. 2. Bandwidth estimation. 3. Filter out estimated signal. Repeat until all transmissions detected. Labels from known capture scenario. DSP labels used for training and test performance metrics.
Raw I/Q Labeled Training 5.2ms DSP Spectrogram. 1s 1s (512x512) Labeling Splitting Capture Capture Spectrograms. Unlabeled Test Spectrogram (512x512) Splitting Spectrograms.

evidence.original_text: Receiver: USRP B205mini SDR. 20 dBi panel antenna with an 18° beamwidth. 6 ft coax cable between Rx and antenna with no additional LNA. Captures: 6, 1s captures per scenario. 50 MHz bandwidth. 2.4435 GHz center frequency – slightly offset from the perfect center of the four, 10 MHz drone channels. DJI controllers’ 1 MHz signals hop over 2.4-2.4835 GHz (ISM band). The captured bandwidth covers the four DJI drone channels (Channel 1, Channel 2, Channel 3, Channel 4) each approximately 10 MHz wide, centered around 2.4185 GHz, 2.4435 GHz, and 2.4685 GHz.

entries.context: RF receiver configuration for UAVSig dataset captures

evidence.original_text: Scenario = one combo of specific TX(s) and channel parameters. Two capture days (5/9/24 and 5/10/24). Full dataset (~222 GB) available at: https://cores.ee.ucla.edu/downloads/datasets/uavsig/. The dataset consists of wideband, multi-channel data from multiple transmitters of the same models. It includes captures from 4 DJI Matrice 100 drones and controllers. The physical setup involved campus building neighboring roofs with the RX antenna angled upward on the upper roof to avoid interference. The dataset provides time-frequency labels generated by a DSP labeling algorithm.

Example Full 1s Capture – Drones. Raw I/Q data from a 1s capture is processed through DSP labeling to produce a labeled 1s capture. This labeled capture is then split into training spectrograms. Each training spectrogram has a duration of 5.2ms and a size of 512x512 pixels. Similarly, unlabeled test spectrograms (512x512) are also split from the captures. The same process is shown for controllers in 'Example Full 1s Capture – Controllers'. The DSP labeling algorithm overview includes an iterative signal detection algorithm involving time-domain duration estimation, bandwidth estimation, and filtering out estimated signal, repeating until all transmissions are detected. Labels from known capture scenario are used, and DSP labels are used for training and test performance metrics.

entries.context: Training spectrogram splitting from labeled 1s captures
